Some feeds do change frequently. The Jam and Compo may be over, but Extra is still ongoing for the next ~2 weeks, so game feeds will continue to be in flux for a while. So yes, Because of that, the 50th item of an offset request might also be 51st item returned.

As for the unusual offsets, some feeds can change frequently. That said, it's rare to see things move really fast (rarely more than 1-2 spaces), so we often grab 5 extra items to handle things shifting around.

So yes, the "proper" way to use the feed functions is to append new items on to the end, EXCEPT when they are already on the list. The timestamps returned by the feed code (node2/feed?) sometimes change, which you can use to tell you if a node's data has been updated before re-fetching it.

Getting a feed of `/tag/platform`, off the top of my head I can't remember exactly what it does, but I think that gives you a list of all games with any platform tags. That's not necessarily all games, as tagging is currently an internal feature used whenever you add a metadata Link to your game. It's not currently used when you upload to Ludum Dare directly. You might also be seeing dupes from someone adding a "platform/source-code" tag then later adding a "platform/windows" tag, an off-site HTML5 Itch version as "platform/web", etc. In theory, any time someone adds a new link to any of the tens of thousands of games on the website, that feed will change.

I believe some feeds do change less, but again, I'm not quite sure what data you're looking for.

Hi @Cerno-b. Like most things, the main roadblock is time, me needing to prioritize other things first, and that there hasn't been much interest from others to tackle this issue. That said this is something I've penciled in for the winter (assuming I can afford to work on it).

Here's a further breakdown of some of the sticky points if you are anyone are interested in helping out.

* User and post data is stored in an older "long unsupported" version of a WordPress dataabase
* Game data is stored in 2 different formats (https://github.com/LudumDare/ludumdare-2008)
  * as blog posts with some special tags (identifying that it's a game but nothing about it)
  * in a proprietary format with actual game details
* Blog and game page data is written in HTML, but the current page and game data is written in Markdown (not 100% Commonmark compliant, but the goal is to make it compliant in time)
  * We will not support HTML on the new website, and the formatting must be converted to Commonmark (or discarded)
* YouTube embeds worked differently across websites
* External image embeds are no longer supported (for security and performance reasons)
* Other embeds (iframes) are not supported
* New website needs to support "other" events (right now it only supports Ludum Dare events as they're currently run, no classic LD or MiniLD style events)
* Because not all data has a matching account, we're going to need dummy/proxy accounts to own much of the data, and a procedure to claim games that belong to you
* GDPR removal requests

I'll update this if I think of anything else, but that's the gist of things.

If I was to pick a big sticking point for me, it would be the HTML -> Commonmark conversion. There are some existing PHP libraries that support this, but they don't do a great job in my testing. The process of converting the data over, as I see it, will be to write some new code that parses the source Post and Game data, spits out tags that it doesn't know what to do with, and we "rinse and repeat" adding support for some of the funny things people did until most of the posts are handled. For example, YouTube embeds I believe were done with `<iframe>`'s, so we can use some clever logic to extract those. Other embeds should just be turned into normal links. Some users also went `<div>` happy or used HTML tables, and it would be nice to see those handled in some reasonable way. Sadly some people did custom formatting with style tags, and IMO those should just be discarded.

The only data we're bringing over is data belonging to users that submitted games: their games and their posts. I've been leaning away from migrating the comments, but that's TBD (also there are multiple comment systems,: wordpress's and the proprietary comments on game pages). Again this would only be comments from people that submitted games.

Minor update: after digging into it there are a long list of reasons why this might not work. One is because of some "Internet Explorer" event bubbling hackery in our TextArea's. The other is due to some bad coding practices (i.e. the TextArea's are changing their own props).

Once the "remove Internet Explorer support" merge is complete, this should be tested again.

One solution is to take advantage of #548, placing a pagination marker. The Twitter mobile app does something like this, where you get a "load more marker" after a block of posts.

IMO that kinda sucks though. If you're not attentive enough, you end up missing the marker, and many tweets as a result.

The Twitter desktop site works around this issue by _always loading everything_. If your queue is 200 tweets, it loads 200 tweets, appending them to the front. This is a desirable solution, but the longer you wait (hours, days), the more content that is requested, and the array of things loaded potentially becomes unbearable.

One more solution is to make the "load more" button at the top discard the entire view contents, effectively making it a refresh button. It gives you an estimate of how many new posts there are. This has the added benefit of only needing to know a number, not the ids themselves for updates.

A final option might be a combination of the last 2. Beyond a certain threshold, offer to refresh the view instead of just loading what's new.

I just pushed out an update (v 2.10.0) that adds support for string.Contains().

But, after another look, I don't think this is going to work at all.  If you create the filter like this:
```
roles.Any(r => x.AllowedRolesPremissionJsonString.Contains(r))
```
that will create a subquery that attempts to evaluate this portion of that query:
```
r => x.AllowedRolesPremissionJsonString.Contains(r)
```
The problem there is that it's accessing the outer table ('x' in the linq query) which would not be valid sql since the inner sql query will not have any reference to the outer "x" entity set.

I also don't know if a valid query can be created against the parameter "roles" - it may only work on an entity collection.

> What is the intended way to deal with different locales and floating point formats an this library

> Shouldn't this library already take care of this an provide proper parsing for any locals?

Library provides a form engine that encapsulates the logic of manipulating with the form state + mechanisms of validators/asyncValidators
All basic valueAccessors are bonus points and serve nicely for kickstarting + as example of implementation.
Proper parsing of any locales is just one from tons of possible edge cases. The library should definitely avoid supporting all of them.

I've checked the bugs, and I could reproduce it.

`UserProfile.jsx` component renders a number of post by accessing `state.global.get('accounts').get(username)`.

But global state fetched and updated with the API response.
So in my opinion, it is out of condensers scope.

@pkattera, If is it still needs to fix it, I can change `UserProfile.jsx`'s logic to calculate based on the `blogs`, not `post_count`
Any updates on this? This behaviour actually causes problems for me atm as we send receipts as encrypted memos and most users just can not see them.

Proposed solution: https://github.com/steemit/condenser/pull/3442

**Known issue:** 
- post a new video on DTube
- edit the post with Steemit or any other dApp
- browse to the post on the Condenser, the label now shows the last dApp from which the post has been edited. This is because the `app` field in the json_metadata is updated with each edit and there is no history of previous dApps used or of the first dApp.

Posts list:
![Screen Shot 2019-07-18 at 7 54 05 am](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/310654/61414839-57eb2d00-a932-11e9-8562-cd8690528d76.jpg)

Post page:
![Screen Shot 2019-07-18 at 7 54 19 am](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/310654/61414842-5a4d8700-a932-11e9-8776-2d9ebd3906e3.jpg)

Nothing about being on the bad actors list is censorship. 

Furthermore, final authority over what does or does not appear on steemit.com rests with Steemit Inc., the owner of that specific domain name; anything else would be censorship via dictating an editorial process. 

If your anti-censorship plan depends on what Steemit does or does not do on steemit.com feature-wise, then your anti-censorship plan is a bad one. :)

Re: @pfunks, we can push updates to steemit.com in minutes - and sometime soon these lists will be runtime dynamic, not hard coded, and won’t require a deploy.

https://github.com/giuseppec/iml/issues/:
One update: this seems to only be an issue when operating in parallel with the BART model. I do not obtain the same importance for all variables when using other models (e.g., Ranger, xgboost, GBM, etc.) in parallel or when using BART with sequential variable importance calculation. I have modified the code such that it will reproduce this issue below:

```
library(mlr3verse)
mlr3extralearners::install_learners("regr.bart")
#library(iml)
#library(future)
n <- 100
set.seed(123)
x1 <- rnorm(n, 4, 5)
x2 <- sample(c("a", "b","c"), size = n, replace = T)
x3 <- sample(letters[1:4], size = n, replace = T)
x4_noise <- rnorm(n, 1, 6)

y <- 3 + 2*x1 + 5*(x2=="a") - 10*(x2=="b") + 25*(x2=="c") +
  4*(x3=="a") - 4*(x3=="b") + 5*(x3=="c") +10*(x3=="d") - 
  50*(x3=="d")*(x2=="b") +
  rnorm(n, 0, 3)

df <- data.frame(x1 = x1, x2 = factor(x2), 
                 x3 = factor(x3), x4_noise = x4_noise, 
                 y = y)
task <- as_task_regr(df, target = "y")
gr <- po("scale") %>>% po("encode") %>>% lrn("regr.bart")
grl <- GraphLearner$new(gr)
grl$train(task)

future::plan("multisession", workers=2)
model <- iml::Predictor$new(grl, data = df, y = "y")
imp_mod <- iml::FeatureImp$new(model, loss = "rmse", 
                               n.repetitions = 50, 
                               compare = "ratio") 
imp_mod$results
```

> Unfortunately {Metrics} has not seen a commit since March 2019 and it looks like it has been abandoned.

I created the issue in the `Metrics` package repo, and he [replied pretty past](https://github.com/mfrasco/Metrics/issues/45#issuecomment-789898338). I also noticed that the package provides an `fbeta_score` function which works for binary classification with `0/1` labels, but not for multi-class classification (e.g., `micro` and `macro` `F1`).

So I think the documentation for [Feature Importance](https://christophm.github.io/iml/reference/FeatureImp.html) can be updated stating more precisely to use `fbeta_score` for binary classification (with `0/1` labels) and may be a custom implementation for multi-class classification.

Hello all,
A further update (and a wave to future-me who will, no doubt, in years to come, need this info because present-me did not document it properly, like in 2022 :) )

I tried to sync a shared (with me) folder locally using the syncfolder script kindly provided by @alworx 
Whereas this works/ed very well with folders in my own account, I could not get the script to accept the Shared folder.
It returned the folder did not exist. 
refused command:  "./syncfolder.sh add /Shared /home/myuser/pcloud_Local/Shared sync" 
probably my mistake.

Using the info in this thread, I manually added the record in the sqlite syncfolder table.
This did work and the sync of the shared folder is up and running.  

steps:
0) run and stop pcloud sync      in order to create the sqlite3 data.db file

1) retrieve local inode, deviceid for the local target folder
$ stat -c '%i %d' /home/myuser/pcloud_Local/Shared
**524920** **2049**

2) retrieve the folderid of the /Shared folder      
Shared = the default pcloud top level folder for files/folders shared with the user  - can by customized
sqlite> select * from folder where name='Shared';
**6639371585**|0|15913130|15|Shared|1594892813|1710944581|0|3

3) add new recorded to syncfolder table
sqlite>insert into syncfolder (id,folderid,localpath,synctype,flags,inode,deviceid)
values( **1**,6639371585,'/home/myuser/pcloud_Local/Shared',3,**0**,524920,2049);

this was the first record so id =1
flag 0 to prime the initial download

4) start the pcloud sync   
pcloudcc -u <pcloud account> -p -d

HTH

@rrahn Yes, currently we jump to the first alignment within the region if any are present (`hasAlignments=true`), or the first alignment after the region(or end of file) if none is present  (`hasAlignments=false`). The latter behaviour is not documented and should in any case be updated. The jumpToRegion function returns true in both cases.

This only works though, because in new versions the linear index gives the file offset of the first alignment in the bin spanning the region (if present) or the offset of the first alignment in a preceding bin. This way we can afterwards advance in the BAM file and check the region again. With 0's as file offsets this doesn't work. So the question is how to handle this case (Correcting the index while reading it in or going back until a valid index entry in another preceeding reference is found, both share the problem of what to do if all preceeding index entry's are invalid/0's).

Update: I just realized that the manufacturer ID of the W25Q128JV is 0xF7, but Winbond is 0xEF in this library.  Regardless, setting a `#define CAPACITY MB(16)` and `flash.begin( CAPACITY );` did not work.

I switched to the stable branch, which seems to change a lot about how the chip ID is handled (Commit 5bb2267f7f090d94389b57a68c8b3e96f7d1dd26).  The W25Q128JV still isn't supported directly, but at least now defining a capacity makes it work.

Not sure if actually reading/writing will work...

### Update 2020-02-24

I found the following piece of code that does a test on SPI Flash for ESP32:
https://github.com/kriswiner/ESP32/blob/master/SPI/SPIFlash_ESP32.ino

This piece of code (single file) doesn't use any library except the default ```#include <SPI.h>```

To my surprise, after changing the line: 
```SPI.begin(18, 19, 23, 5); // sck, miso, mosi, ss (ss can be any GPIO)```  
to 
```SPI.begin(14, 12, 13, 15); // sck, miso, mosi, ss (ss can be any GPIO)```

The all the tests (Write / Read / Erase / Read again) worked flawlessly.

It is difficult for me to debug, as my only tool is **Serial.println**,  but I suspect the chip identification in _getJedecId (SPIFlashIO.cpp).  I might be totally wrong so I don't want to waste your time with false tracs. 

I will continue investigation on my side.
https://github.com/purcell/envrc/issues/: With only `envrc-global-mode` enabled I cannot reproduce a recursive call to `envrc--update` (there is only one). And with the global mode off and `prog-mode-hook` including `envrc-mode`, `envrc--update` is also triggered only once.

This has since been fixed, which is great, but it means that there is now no longer a "public" way to trigger `envrc--update-env` without a potentially-slow `direnv reload`.

It's useful to be able to trigger it e.g. when you change directories in a `vterm` without having to run a global update or pay the cost of `direnv reload`.

Hmmm, this would perhaps be a good case for adding a buffer-local hook like "envrc-post-update-hook", then you could use it to sync changes from updated env vars into emacs variables, as in your example.

@oliverlee Since lambda expressions aren't allowed in constant expressions until C++17, PR #311 probably wouldn't be useful without more C++14 fixes, so yes, I think it's reasonable to update PR #311 rather than creating a new PR.

That being said, you might prefer to consult with the other maintainers of this repository -- @crtrott , @dalg24 , @nmm0 , and others -- before setting out to do a lot of work.

https://github.com/aviator-co/av/issues/:
Been thinking about this — one possible direction is a per-branch marker on the children rather than a config-level "additional trunks" list (which was the closed #286 approach).

A `--remote-trunk` flag at branch creation:

```bash
av branch --remote-trunk big-v2-update pr1
```

`pr1`'s metadata records that its parent is a *boundary* — the remote-tracking ref `<configured-remote>/big-v2-update`. Then:

- When av walks the parent chain to find the stack root, it stops at this boundary (treats it like a trunk).
- "Rebase to trunk" semantics target the remote-trunk for this stack, not `main`. So `av sync --rebase-to-trunk` from `pr3` rebases `pr1`/`pr2`/`pr3` onto the latest `<remote>/big-v2-update`, not onto `main`. The remote-trunk *is* trunk for this stack.
- av never tries to push, restack, merge-detect, or delete `big-v2-update` itself — it's outside av's management for this stack.
- When `pr1` is merged into `big-v2-update` on GitHub, you `git fetch`, then `av sync` rebases `pr2`/`pr3` onto the new tip. The "PR is already merged but local points elsewhere → wants to delete" failure mode goes away because av no longer thinks `big-v2-update` is part of the stack.

Same mechanism covers another case I've been hitting — stacking on a **coworker's** branch:

```
main
 └── origin/coworker-feature   (PR #100, coworker's)
      └── my-backend           (mine, --remote-trunk coworker-feature)
           └── my-frontend     (mine)
```

Here the boundary protects the coworker's branch from being silently restacked/pushed by `av sync`.

Both cases reduce to the same thing: this remote-tracking ref is my stack's effective trunk, don't touch it.

I'm ok with checking that the local and remote branches have the same commits. I'm currently setting the remote for the branch, pulling the update, then resyncing. Something to reduce those steps would be nice.

We rebase only topic branches on top of the latest master on the remote side. You would notice the remote tracking branch is always updated after `av stack sync`. We can add an option to rebase the local master, but right now the CLI is not bothering local master as it's not relevant.

Thank you for your confirmation @draftcode. I have working on the solution but i just want to confirm if my approach is correct. In your comment above

> I guess we can check if the branch order is the same as the current local branch order. This won't detect all the cases that GH would recognize there's no new commit, but should cover some.

I wonder if after we find out that the orders are different between local and remote, then what is the suggested next step? 

My thought based on my experiment at https://github.com/wahyudibo/test-aviator-av/pull/1:  since we're updating PR before pushing it to github, 

- Catch `error: failed to update pull request: github error: There are no new commits between base branch 'frontend-change' and head branch 'backend-1'` error using conditional `if`
- append the branch with those error to a temporary array 
- iterate the temporary array to fix the PR after `vm.runGitPush()` is executed. 

In my testing repo, i create 2 branch stack (main > backend-1 > frontend-change), reorder them using `av stack reorder` so the order changes (main > frontend-change > backend-1) then run `av stack sync` using modified binary where the above error is skipped so the cli is not stopped. The result is both `frontend-change` and `backend-1` base branches in the PR are pointed to `main`. This condition can be fixed if we run `av stack sync` once again. This time `backend-1` branch in the PR correctly points to frontend-change instead of `main`. 

Please let me know if this makes sense or not and if you have further suggestion.

I tracked the problem down to this line:
https://github.com/aviator-co/av/blob/9b993bb53f138f6fe1af37605dacbbabd4f90ae7/internal/git/git.go#L41

If you have a repo that had a previous default branch name. Like in my case our repo used to have a default branch named `master`, and then you switch to `main`, the output of the command `git symbolic-ref refs/remotes/origin/HEAD` will still point to the original branch name.

If I run the below command:
```
⇒  git symbolic-ref refs/remotes/origin/HEAD refs/remotes/origin/main
⇒  git symbolic-ref refs/remotes/origin/HEAD
refs/remotes/origin/main
```

Then you can see that my `symbolic-ref` has been updated correctly also.

Maybe this should have a check for this error case? Thanks!

Hi @Abhimaurya and @superderon,
Yes the vehicle counting algorithm uses a simple tracker called [CvBlob](https://code.google.com/p/cvblob/), and it is not robust for multiple tracking purpose.
Some nice algorithms for multiple tracking are [Multiple Hypothesis Tracking](https://github.com/WeatherGod/MHT) and [Particle Filter](https://github.com/NewProggie/Particle-Filter).
I will try to replace the CvBlob for a more robust tracking algorithm in the future.
Please let me updated if you have some advances in this direction.
Best,
Andrews

https://github.com/RailsEventStore/ecomm:
My idea how to achieve it:
1. Make `Price` an independent read model with `prices` table, which would be associated to the `Product`. This would substitute the `current_prices_calendar` attribute. An entry in `prices`, beside the reference to a product, would have `valid_from` and `value` attributes. We could also add a `currency` and build a value object for Money.
2. Prices will have their identities, which will allow us to update or delete them.

Alternatively, we would need to edit the whole `current_prices_calendar` whenever we want to update the prices, because the entries in the calendar have no identities. This solution looks to me like a workaround.

There is also one issue with the `current_prices_calendar`. I don't see a logic to cut off the past prices. So it does not really contain the `current` prices, but just all the prices.
https://github.com/networkservicemesh/ne:
I got sidetracked from this issue as it wasn't really a blocker. However I got to move to nsm v0.1.0 tag and also reproduced it. 
I was also able to fix it based on the issues seen. And was able to run a repeat loop for 30 times without issues. 

The patch addresses 3 things. 2 panics in nsmd. And a lockup in vpp server which seems to be a result of a configurator race from dataplane from Request and Close. As the lockup in vpp results in vpp connect failures and dataplane failing with ErrConnectionFailed to network service manager. vppctl also hangs on unix socket on a connect when this happens.
With a mutex synchronizing ConnectOrDisconnect, this issue of vpp lockup does not seem to occur.
And dataplane configuration seems to be successful on repeat install and delete test case.

However need to confirm if the next 2 patches in nsmd as mentioned below would prevent the vpp lockup (highly unlikely)

First one is simple and based on a missing networkservice endpoint resulting in traversal of null endpoints 
in RestoreConnections. This issue won't be present in v0.2.0. 
However the second panic stems from an interface typecast error while trying to close a local connection with a local network service client for a remote destination connection and vice-versa for a remote network service client with local destination/connection. 

This was from Upate cross connects. 
Need to check if this can be hit.

The FileNotFoundError was caused due to an incorrect file path. The image california.png is located in machine_learning/images/end_to_end_project/, but the code was trying to load it from images/end_to_end_project/ (missing the machine_learning/ prefix).

I updated the file path in the code to:
california_img = mpimg.imread('machine_learning/images/end_to_end_project/california.png')

After this correction, the file loads successfully and the code runs without errors.
This resolves the issue, so it can be closed now.

Hello @cfbauer ,
Your error is because the script is trying to setup the dark mode and for this it need to read the user.reg file (generated automatically when you select the Windows 10 version) in: $PATH_OF_INSTALLATION/PhotoshopCC/user.reg but it can't do it for some reason.

For solve some of your problems, I updated the script for optimize some things and for example to ask us if we want the dark version. **Also one of the things that I'm happier is that now the script don't ask anymore to click in the Windows libraries for install, now the process is automatic and when Photoshop is installed, it opens in the background and the script asks you well if you want to return to the back of the program or exit.**

So in that case, for solve your problem and for let us to decide if we want the dark mode, the script now asks if we want the dark mode. If you want simply install Photoshop, tell the script that you don't won't it.
But if you want the dark mode, when the script asks to you if you want the dark mode, check if the user.reg file that I mention before exists. If not, for some reason Wine is not creating the file.

**Thanks for sharing your errors and help to others!!
I'm waiting to know if with the new version works for you.**

Chosen 3.0 was just released, so I'm going to look into the updates for any accessibility improvements. The developer also responded to feedback on another issue regarding aria labeling, so I'm hopeful we're still able to progress without switching plugins.

Just a minor update:

4.2 does include the "facade" bits I mentioned for the logger, so you can get either the standard Laminas logger or one that implements the PSR logger interface (it's still the Laminas logger underneath). It also is using a fork of ours of laminas-log to offer 8.4 support. That should all hold true for the 4.2 series at least.

On the "bigger" level, I don't see us moving to Symfony any time soon, simply due to the amount of work that's likely to create for module developers (and core developers, for that matter). For laminas-mvc, I'm increasingly of the opinion that we'll use a fork there. laminas-mvc is/was quite stable and I don't foresee it being a significant maintenance burden.

The updated contrast looks good, and the amended alerts certainly lend more clarity to the process!

There may be a larger discussion in all of this for considering moving away from disabled buttons and replacing them with, as one example of an alternative, operable buttons that produce error messages when clicked that require confirmation by the user. Alternatively, tooltips could potentially be added to include instruction to the button, but I could see how this may remove some of the intentionality behind having the two step confirmation process.

In this case, however, I do not believe `disabled` reveals the button to screenreader users as it is unfocusable (tested with VoiceOver on Chrome, Firefox, and Safari). `disabled` should remain to inform the style of disabled buttons, but an `aria-disabled` attribute would also need to be introduced to bring that focus back. Can that be done here? @kimisgold has pointed out the possible overhaul required in introducing `aria-disabled`, depending on how it is done, so I am curious (especially thinking a bit more globally about how disabled buttons are used across S) what would be the best approach in terms of clarifying the process to screenreader users while understanding what is actually plausible in the code here.

Thanks for the repro steps. Canta itself crashing (rather than the uninstall silently failing) points at something in the Shizuku call path — most likely the binder dropping mid-operation or an exception Canta doesn't handle. To pin it down I need the crash trace:

1. Reproduce the crash (authorize Canta, delete an app).
2. Capture logcat right after: `adb logcat -d > canta_crash.txt` — or in Shizuku+, the built-in log viewer around the time of the crash.
3. Paste the stack trace here (the frames mentioning `Canta`, `shizuku`, or `Binder`).

One thing to try meanwhile: update to **v13.6.0.r2139+** and re-authorize Canta once (off/on). Several binder-delivery/registration bugs were fixed recently (#319, #335), and a stale binder is a plausible cause for a crash specifically *during* a privileged uninstall. Let me know if it still crashes on the latest build + the trace.

Just to confirm drop-in is supposed to be paired with an installation of Rikka Shizuku?

Found the actual root cause — it's not specific to Magic Tuner, it affects any app authorized through the normal toggle switch (which is how nearly everyone authorizes apps).

`updateFlagsForUid()` (the server code behind the toggle switch) was recording an **empty package list** for every authorization it created — only the separate interactive permission-request dialog flow ever recorded real package names. Two places then misread "empty list" as "covers nothing" instead of "nothing recorded yet":

1. The authorized-apps list query excludes a package from the list entirely (not just shows it as unauthorized) if the uid's recorded package list doesn't contain it — and an empty list never contains anything, so the app vanishes from the list on the very next refresh.
2. The server's own startup reconciliation (runs every time the Shizuku+ server restarts — device reboot, watchdog restart, or an OEM background-killing your server, which several "tuner"/battery-manager apps are known to do aggressively) treated the empty list as proof the uid's packages changed, and deleted the whole authorization entry outright.

That second part is why your re-toggle-then-reinstall didn't fix it for good: each re-toggle recreated the same unpopulated entry, so the next server restart wiped it again. It also explains why it "keeps" disappearing rather than just failing once.

Fixed in `b2b248c2` (**v13.6.0.r2169**, just released): the toggle path now records real package names, and — so nobody has to manually re-toggle every affected app — existing broken entries self-heal from the live package list the next time the server restarts.

Could you update to r2169+ and let me know if Magic Tuner (or anything else that was disappearing) stays put now? A server restart (or just leaving the phone to naturally cycle it) should be enough to trigger the self-heal even without re-toggling.

Looked into this. `IInterface.asBinder()` on a null reference means InstallerX got a **null binder back from Shizuku** and then called `.asBinder()` on it — i.e. the Shizuku service binder never reached InstallerX's process, rather than the install call itself being rejected. (If a Plus feature had actively blocked the call, you'd see a `SecurityException: Binder Firewall...`, not an NPE — and both the Binder Firewall and Shadow Binder features are off by default, so unless you turned one on, they're not involved here.)

That points at the same registration/delivery problem behind #319, which just got two fixes:
- **`b6be301c`** (r2118+): if you have both Shizuku+ and the Drop-In build installed, the server only trusted one of them, so the other's clients silently failed to receive the binder.
- **`a42edcac`** (just pushed): the server cached a *failed first* binder-push to an app for its whole process lifetime — so if the push raced ahead of the app being ready, that app stayed without a working Shizuku binder until relaunched. Now it retries.

Could you:
1. Update to the next build (r2119+) and retry an install with InstallerX — ideally after force-stopping InstallerX once so it re-requests the binder cleanly.
2. Confirm whether you have **both** Shizuku+ and Drop-In installed, or just one (and which).
3. If it still fails, paste the **full stack trace** from your screenshot as text — the frames below `asBinder()` will show whether the null is InstallerX's Shizuku handle or something deeper.

"Worked on the other fork, broke on ShizukuPlus" fits this: the registration path is where ShizukuPlus diverged most, and it was genuinely buggy until these two fixes.

Your logs show a **watchdog crash-restart loop** on r2070, but a Manual Crash Report without the underlying stack trace doesn't tell me *what* is crashing — and r2070 is quite far back now.

A large batch of crash fixes have landed since, several directly in the service/binder start path that your logs are looping on:
- r2118 — `IShizukuApplication` AIDL descriptor mismatch (granted apps / callbacks)
- r2129 — Android 17 support
- r2139 — stock-client permission grants
- r2142 — update-install crash

Please update to **r2142+** and see if the loop clears. If it still crash-loops, grab a fresh report or `adb logcat` around the moment of the crash and paste the stack trace here — with the actual exception I can pinpoint it. Leaving open pending that.

This is almost certainly **One UI 8 killing the accessibility service** — Samsung is the most aggressive OEM here, and A16/One UI 8 tightened background limits further. The tell is that AICore+ stays enabled while the a11y service drops: they're bound through different mechanisms, so the OS reaping the a11y service specifically fits OEM power management rather than an app crash.

Things that usually stop it:
1. **Battery optimization → Unrestricted** for Shizuku+ (Settings ▸ Battery ▸ Background usage limits — make sure Shizuku+ isn't in "Sleeping/Deep sleeping apps").
2. **Lock Shizuku+ in Recents** (pull down on its card).
3. Disable **"Remove permissions if app is unused"** for Shizuku+ (App info ▸ pause app activity if unused → off).

Also please update from **r2070** — you're ~70 builds behind, and a lot of service-lifecycle fixes have landed since (latest is r2142). If the a11y service still drops on the latest build *with* battery optimization off and the app locked in recents, tell me and I'll look at hardening the keep-alive path.

@Scoop2389 — thanks, that logcat and your precise repro nailed it.

The "0 authorized apps until you reopen the list" (and the 0-after-reboot / 0-after-toggle-all) was a **race in the home screen's count refresh**: several refreshes fire at once — one when the service connects, one on screen resume — each computing the count on a background thread and writing whichever finishes last. A slower earlier pass that computed **0** (before the server was fully serving app data, or before a just-applied grant had propagated) could land *after* the correct count and overwrite it. Fixed in **v13.6.0.r2136+** (latest is r2138) with a generation guard so only the freshest pass is allowed to update the display — the count should now populate on launch and update immediately after toggling.

Important distinction on the grants themselves: your list showing the right number once reopened confirms the **grants do persist across reboot** — it was purely the home-screen counter that was stale, not the permissions. If on r2136+ you ever see a genuine loss of *granted* permissions (not just a wrong counter) after a reboot, tell me and I'll dig into server-side persistence separately.

Still chasing the Obtainium detection issue — that's part of a deeper **Compat Hub** pass I'm working on right now. Really appreciate you testing on A17 stable. 🙏

That said, your build (`13.6.0.r2033`) matches several Android 16 crashes that were fixed in the latest build (commit `27667aeb`) — a cluster of foreground-service crashes (an internal automation monitor was using an FGS type Android 16 rejects/time-limits), a Compose settings-search crash, and a few R8-related keep-rule issues. Any one of those could have been the culprit on a OneUI 8 / Android 16 device.

Could you update once the next release is out and see if it recurs? If it does, the in-app crash log (Settings → About → last crash report, or wherever it's surfaced) capturing a **fresh** crash would let us pin down the exact cause. Aiming to close this out once you've had a chance to confirm on the new build. 🙏

Thanks for the extremely detailed writeup — I want to flag something important before this turns into a big OEM-specific rewrite, because I think the actual root cause here is much simpler than the theory in this issue.

**`kd.a` is not a vivo/OriginOS obfuscated framework class — it's an R8-minified class name.** R8 (the code shrinker/obfuscator every release build goes through) renames classes to short identifiers like this by default; `kd.a`, `a2`, etc. are exactly what R8 output looks like, on every device, regardless of OEM. It isn't evidence of a vivo-specific serialization format that needs a custom adapter.

`BadParcelableException: ClassNotFoundException when unmarshalling: kd.a` is the *exact* signature of a bug that was already found and fixed this week (#298, #306): the manager's ProGuard config replaces Android's default rules instead of extending them, so R8 was stripping the `CREATOR` field (and in some cases the whole class identity) off the `BinderContainer` Parcelable the server sends over the binder handshake. That's unrelated to vivo/OriginOS specifically — it happened on Android 12 (S24) devices too. It's fixed in commits `374edbe3` and `dd8e47a1`, already in the latest build.

Given that, before building an entire OriginOS/Axeron-style compatibility layer: could you update to the latest release and re-test on the iQOO Z10? If `kd.a` was the actual class being unmarshalled (rather than a genuinely vivo-specific class), this should now work without any OEM-specific code. If it *still* fails after updating, that's much stronger evidence there's a real OriginOS-specific issue underneath, and at that point a fresh logcat capturing the exact failing class name post-fix would tell us whether it's something new. Appreciate the research either way — the Axeron reference is a good pointer if it does turn out to be device-specific.

Update: found and fixed one more layer under #319 today (a dual-flavor manager-recognition gap in the server — see the comment on #319 for details, commit `b6be301c`/r2118). If you have more than one ShizukuPlus flavor installed, or are still seeing registration/detection issues on the latest build, that's worth checking next.

To note, the app might need fixing on my end — I am in the process of improving the current state of the application and will provide more updates soon.

 — thejaustin

Systemless OTA Payload verification has been secured. We implemented the OtaSafetyValidator package which strictly parses the META-INF/com/android/metadata inside OTA zips. It now cryptographically enforces that the payload's pre-device codename matches the physical device, and detects CSC data-wipe flags to actively warn the user before handing the payload to update_engine.

Hi @mchlbowyer,

I had a chance to take a look and I don't have good news for now. In order to support Mac Catalyst we need two static libraries compiled for Mac but for different targets. It should be done for every dependency. The problem is these libraries cannot be merged using lipo because both of them will have x86_64 arch. I could use different build conditions for iOS and macOS targets but it would break cocoapods because it doesn't support such configuration.

The solution is to use new framework format `xcframework`. All necessary archs can be combined there. The only thing is we have to wait till cocoapods devs update their tool and add support of this format. And hopefully, it won't affect min target version as I still want to support iOS 9.

>I don't know if installing Saxi via NPM is conflicting with me running the fork somehow.

It depends where you installed it.  If you attempted to install the NPM package _in your cloned repository_, the dependencies in `node_modules` will not match the cloned code and it won't work.  `npm install` will update the dependencies to match those.  If you installed it in some other folder, it will have a totally separate `node_modules` folder and they won't conflict.  That said, if you're intending to play with code changes I recommend _not_ installing from NPM.  It will only cause confusion, and is meant for end-user that just want to install and run unmodified.

Took Fable for a spin this evening to dig through this - it wrote the telemetry harness and then I A/B tested against the machine until we had something sensible to look at in the numbers.

**TLDR: The stutter is the plotter literally running out of buffered motion - the EBB's motion FIFO is 1 command deep, so any hiccup on the host longer than one motion block (~10-20ms on dense files) physically stalls the steppers. Fix: EBB firmware 3.x supports a deeper FIFO (`CU,4`) - with 32+ commands buffered on the board the plotter glides straight through host stalls. PR: https://github.com/alexrudd2/saxi/pull/305**

Finally got to the bottom of this, with telemetry added to saxi (timing every LM command against its planned block duration, sampling QM, logging GC pauses / event-loop stalls, plus the firmware 2.8.1 `CU,3` trick that turns the red USR LED into a live "FIFO empty" indicator).

### Setup
- saxi 0.17.1 (alexrudd2/saxi)
- EBB firmware 2.8.1 -> 3.0.3 (note: AxiDraw Control 3.9.5 reports 2.8.1 as "up to date" - it predates the 3.x firmware line, you have to flash manually from the EggBot repo)
- AxiDraw Control 3.9.5
- Windows 10 Pro (10.0.19045), Node v24.16.0

### What's happening
- My longPlot.svg test file (one continuous line, same one from the dropbox folder above) plans into ~66k motion blocks of ~18ms each. Every block is one `LM` serial command, and saxi waits for the OK before sending the next.
- The EBB's motion FIFO is 1 command deep at boot. So if the host pauses for longer than ~18ms, the board finishes what it has and physically stops - that's the stutter. And the host pauses *a lot*: telemetry caught Node GC pauses and event-loop stalls of 50-800ms all through every plot.
- This is why long continuous paths are the trigger - they produce thousands of short blocks back to back, so the command stream is timing-critical for minutes at a time. Disconnected lines get pen-lifts which hide the gaps. And it's why Inkscape simplify "helps" (longer blocks) but changes the drawing.

### Versions matter
- On 0.17.x with the old 2.8.1 firmware things were still not working well - stutters through the whole plot.
- After updating the EBB to firmware 3.0.3, the 0.17.x fixes made a clear improvement, but didn't fix it entirely: episodic stutter remained, and the timestamps lined up exactly with logged GC pauses on the host.

### Findings
A/B on the same file, same machine, same GC load (the deeper FIFO is set with `CU,4`, which only exists in firmware 3.x):

- **FIFO depth 1:** stutters from the start, 2,627 micro-stalls totaling 13.7s, QM caught the FIFO empty, red LED flickering with every hiccup
- **FIFO depth 32:** smooth, +0.4% vs planned duration, zero machine-idle samples in 132 QM polls - same GC pauses in the log, the machine just sails through them

A measured 99ms GC pause mid-plot at depth 32 had no visible effect at all. The same pause at depth 1 is a visible hiccup.

### Fix
https://github.com/alexrudd2/saxi/pull/305 makes saxi set the FIFO to the board's max (`QU,2`) at plot start when firmware >= 3.0.0, and also fixes the post-cancel homing grind (HM was being sent while the board was still executing buffered motion - that one's been grinding the carriage against the end stops for a while I suspect). Older firmware: no behaviour change, the FIFO simply isn't configurable below 3.0.0.

So if you're hitting this: update the EBB to 3.0.3 and run the PR build. The telemetry harness is in the PR too (`SAXI_TELEMETRY=1`) if you want to see your own numbers.

for those who come across this - there is no archive provided by arduino.

your only resort is to cross-compile gcc for the gcc-arm-none-eabi target specifically for the raspberry pi. you can download the source archive here where you can must have a spare raspberry pi with the gcc development environment installed. it requires a lot of patience and sufficient file space to complete the compile (15GB free space, 1GB swap required).

https://developer.arm.com/tools-and-software/open-source-software/developer-tools/gnu-toolchain/gnu-rm/downloads

i just completed it on my own raspberry pi (ran into a lot of issues) - here are the steps i went through. there was an issue compiling gdb, but i simply skipped over it as we do not require it for the arduino development.

```
>> install gcc dependencies

$ sudo apt-get update
$ sudo apt-get install m4 bison flex texinfo texlive

>> increase swap file to 1GB

$ sudo dphys-swapfile swapoff
$ sudo vi /etc/dphys-swapfile           --> CONF_SWAPSIZE=1024
$ sudo dphys-swapfile setup
$ sudo dphys-swapfile swapon

>> compile

$ ./install-sources.sh --skip_steps=mingw32
$ ./build-prerequisites.sh --skip_steps=mingw32
$ ./build-toolchain.sh --skip_steps=mingw32 --build_type=native
```

good luck :) thankfully i have 7-2017q4 pre-compiled binaries on my system now.

I recently went through the learning process of tracking down the DRVSTR settings, so including that keyword might help the name be self documenting. How about:

OUTPUT_DRVSTR0 being the same as OUTPUT, setting low current mode and
OUTPUT_DRVSTR1 being the high output mode?

Googling "samd DRVSTR" immediately leads to answers today...

In any case, it should be implemented before the next release. Sandeep's (wise I think) update from a bitwise to a register write means pinMode(pin,OUTPUT) will now force pin into low current mode. Following immediately with pinStr(pin,HIGH) (my function that does the obvious) would still result in a very brief window where pin was not set to source/sink the high current it is physically connected to. With the current release's bitwise settings I can run pinStr(pin,HIGH) first before pinMode(pin,OUTPUT) and avoid that exposure.

On the practical side, pulling 5 mA out of a pin on DRVSTR0 on a Feather M0 doesn't seem to have damaged anything. The voltage dropped to 2.5 from 3.3, but many seconds in this state didn't prevent operating OK once the fault was fixed.

Well said, Gavin. I completely missed the "return the error message as JSON" layer and (erroneously) focused on the idea of allowing ACT routes to bypass whitelisting. Muh bad there dude. 

That said, after thinking about this more, I can see a couple avenues to lower friction while keeping security intact out of the box. 

1. Allow a list of specific ACT ids to bypass throttling. So it'd be up to developer discretion if they wanted to have specific ACTs without oversight. Think, config file change/update.

2. An extension hook to allow for programmatic exclusion of any/all requests when it comes to throttling. Somehting like, `should_throttle` or something

Another option, though would require some elbow grease in development (for Add-on devs), would be to programmatically add the user's IP address to the configured allow list. The previously mentioned PR for an allow list has been merged (Yay!) and it works through the config layer. https://github.com/ExpressionEngine/ExpressionEngine/pull/4679

@intoeetive yes, but encoding the entry_id alone is not enough. The ID's are sequential, they are predictable as they are auto-incremented from the database. Many EE users might use ID's somewhere in their visible URL segments or site design in general, which means they are exposed, and they are also exposed in other EE forms already everywhere. The ID's alone are not a security measure.

Even if that was not the case, abusers can just try all possible numbers to insert spam. This is why you need 2 values and I used the URI as an example, while you could use the action URL I am not convinced that is good for designers that need the action URL visible for development in the HTML, so it's best just to use the URI value which also matches a segment in the URL. Actually, any backend side on EE that checks the segment or URL will work.

Correct me if I'm wrong, but if the URI generated in the comment form, always matches the segment, and it's always generated by the comment form, then it can be used, otherwise a URL or segment has to be used for this second value beside the entry ID. This should be a value that is always generated before rendering the form.

You mentioned it's used for emails, but this is not actually relevant here, since we are not going to use this for any function except to create the unique hash. The idea is the URI matches the segment/url on which the comment form is generated, which means now in addition to the ID the check results in:

**Is the comment data POST coming from the URL we generate the comment form on? YES**
**Is the comment POST going to the entry ID we want it posted? YES**

If both matches, the comment is accepted. 

If one of them does not, the comment is not accepted. 

Since both values are generated on the backend server side, they are unique and safe, and if the user tampers with them on the front end source code, it's now irrelevant, they are just placeholders because the EE PHP backend is checking for them. 

This should also be changed on each page reload to avoid fixed hashes which can be copied.

Here is a simple code I just created that could easily adapt to the EE before the comment form is generated, adding a new hidden value, and then just checking this after the comment is received. The hash here is unique, a new one each time you load the page. The hash could be used with a native hash function that EE probably and uses in other parts, and the backend most likely already has a URI similar as well.

```
$entry_id = '232'; // This is generated by the EE back end before rendering the EE comment tag on a site template
$URI = 'updates/software-update/just-an-example-url-segment'; // This is generated by the EE back end before rendering the EE comment tag on a site template

$dynamic_data = time(); // current timestamp for my test, use some other unique EE key to make the hash dynamic can be used here....

$raw_string = $entry_id . $URI . $dynamic_data; // We need both values, one alone is not enough

$hash_value = hash('sha256', $raw_string); // We hash both values, use any hash method. A faster option is preferable since it is not something very security sensitive

//echo "Hash Value : " . $hash_value; // When using the {exp:comment} tag in a comment form, this value will be also put as a hidden field

# The user now hits submit on the front end template, POST is data is now processed by EE

# On the EE backend that process comments we check the received value:
$received_hash = 'randomhashbecauseserchangedsomething'; // The user changed the hidden value on either URI or entry_id on the form.

// Check if the re-created hash matches the received hash
if($hash_value === $received_hash){
    echo "Success: Hashes match!"; // Process further EE code that inserts comments into the database
} else {
    echo "Error: Hashes do not match!"; // Exit all code and do not process comment, show an error on the front end
}
```

While URLs are technically case-sensitive, it's generally considered best practice to strictly use lower-case. example.com/foo and example.com/Foo producing different results would be unexpected by most people. Further, ExpressionEngine is sometimes ran on case-insensitive file storage systems, in which case you'd have an error trying to create a differently cased template, or would simply break the site if you started on a case-sensitive system and then moved to case-insensitive.

That said, I agree that if you made a casing error initially, you should be able to update its own name according to your casing preference. This is likely due to MySQL's case insensitivity by default.

I figured out what the problem is (described at the bottom), and put together a workaround.

> [!IMPORTANT]
> This workaround requires disabling the updater. Any updates will have to be done manually.

> [!CAUTION]
> This service gives `figma.com` access to your `local filesystem`.
> **_While highly unlikely_, failing to update could result in exposing your machine to unpatched vulnerabilities.**

**1. Disable and Stop the Updater**
```
systemctl --user disable figma-fonthelper-updater.service
systemctl --user stop figma-fonthelper-updater.service
```

**2. Hard-code your `.local/share` dir in the service file:**
```diff
  [Unit]
  Description=Font Helper for Figma
  After=systemd-user-sessions.service

  [Service]
  Type=simple
- ExecStart=${XDG_CONFIG_HOME}/figma-fonthelper/fonthelper
+ ExecStart=/home/YOUR_USERNAME/.local/share/figma-fonthelper/fonthelper
  ExecStop=/bin/bash -c '`which killall` fonthelper'
  Restart=on-failure

  [Install]
  WantedBy=multi-user.target
```

**3. Restart the systemd daemon and the service**
```
systemctl --user daemon-restart
systemctl --user restart figma-fonthelper.service
```

> [!TIP]
> **You might also want to make sure there are no rogue updaters still running**:
> ```
> $ ps -ef | grep figma
> ```
> ```
> usernam+  927970       0  0 01:21 ?        00:00:00 /bin/bash /home/username/.local/share/figma-fonthelper/updater.sh
> usernam+  936562    1631  0 01:38 ?        00:00:00 /home/username/.local/share/figma-fonthelper/fonthelper
> ```
> ```
> $ kill -9 927970
> ```

------

## The Problem

There are multiple reasons why this isn't working properly...
1. The systemd service files are referencing `XDG_CONFIG_HOME`, when they should be looking for `XDG_DATA_HOME`
2. If either `XDG_DATA_HOME` or `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` are unset, the service will fail anyways
3. The updater is in an infinite loop downloading the latest version. This is due to `fonthelper -v` always returning `0.1.5` even for version `0.1.7`.

That only disables the helper. After reading the installer, I also deleted the following directories and their contents:

~/.local/share/figma-fonthelper
~/.config/figma-linux/
~/.config/systemd/user/figma-fonthelper.service
~/.config/systemd/user/figma-fonthelper-updater.service

> [@Skyyy777](https://github.com/Skyyy777) [@getdir](https://github.com/getdir)
> 
> Unfortunately I don't have any great ideas. I would try deleting all the files and services associated with the previous installation. I believe (on my machine) these are the locations to delete
> 
> ```
> rm -rf /opt/FontHelper
> rm -rf /etc/figma-linux
> rm /usr/lib/systemd/system/fonthelper-updater.service
> rm /usr/lib/systemd/system/fonthelper.service
> ```
> 
> Then try installing fresh:
> 
> ```
> curl -L https://raw.githubusercontent.com/louispotok/figma-linux-font-helper/master/res/install.sh | bash
> ```

update:
Apologies for hijacking, but my case seems relevant, even though we're running with connection pool.
Also [this](https://github.com/ostinelli/apnotic/issues/119) seems related to connection timeouts 

After some digging, it seems that the issue might be related to `net-http2`'s logic here

```
# /lib/net-http2/client.rb

def call(method, path, options={})
  request = prepare_request(method, path, options)
  ensure_open
  new_stream.call_with request
end
```

Right now even if `ensure_open` failed and ended up running the code in`rescue EOFError`, emitting the `on(:error)` for the Apnotic connection, the `new_stream.call_with request` will still be called and will wait for the whole timeout duration 🤔

So, I've been digging around in the client and have begun to understand the update methodology for movement.  

From experimentation, I've adjusted the MOVE_INTERVAL and TIME_THRESHOLD values a bit so I've got some understanding of the appreciable outcome.  

This _minimum_ is basically the average.  And currently is set to 1000ms delta from server returned time.

What this means to the server(based on the way the prediction works) is you are moving until your updates stop.    But since you are sending a packet to the server every 1second and it's transmitting it, you're always 1second behind your current location to observers.

Effectively this means Melee is a game of guesswork and luck, but also why range plays such a huge role in weapon usefulness.

We should look at the fix for lag drift to make combat feel better first. 

This fix would make Meridian much harder but it has been used for years now and players have shown they can deal with the adjustments. 

The backlash and effect here would be similar to the now fixed aggro check limit, as this is a similar bug introduced in the Revelations update many years ago - but I think the effect on gameplay would even out the difficulty and reduce the effectiveness of players who like to build with 90 mules and ruin the entire balance of the server every time they appear.

https://github.com/Dakkshin/after-effect:
The blank `mcp-bridge-auto` panel (and the duplicate floating window some of us see on startup) on AE 2025/2026 comes from two ScriptUI issues — and docking actually works once they're fixed:

**1.** The script always calls `new Window("palette", …)`, so when AE restores the panel from a saved workspace as a docked Panel, the dock host (`this`) is ignored and stays blank while a separate floating window opens. Standard fix:

```js
var panel = (this instanceof Panel) ? this : new Window("palette", "MCP Bridge Auto", undefined);
```

**2.** `panel.update()` (in `executeCommand`) only exists on Window, not on a docked Panel — so once docked it throws and command handling stops. Guard it:

```js
if (panel instanceof Window) { panel.update(); }
```

Also gate `panel.center()/show()` to the Window case, and lay out the host otherwise (`panel.layout.layout(true)` + an `onResizing` handler).

With those, the panel renders and polls correctly when docked on AE 2025/2026 (tested on 2025), so the floating-only fallback isn't actually required. Hope this helps!

Something similar to this has been observed with the update of klipper-helm to include helm v4.

We expect that running `helm install` to upgrade a chart, without making any changes to the chart or values, will be detected as a no-op by helm, and not create a new release. Unfortunately, helm v4 added a new `modtime` field to the files and templates snapshots stored within the chart release. Even if there is no other change to the chart or deployed Kubernetes resources, helm will create a new release to add this field.

I would have expected the recently added protections around job settling to handle this, but it seems there is some gap in that, perhaps related to the active helm controller moving from one node to another during the upgrade.

We will need to add additional logic to the controller to ensure that the installed release is what we expect when the job is complete, so that we can kick off a new update to the job if necessary.

> You said you wanted "the successful installation or update message". This is command output, and currently only available in the pod log. You could modify the script that runs in the pod to also tee it to the termination message file, but even if you did that you'd still need to get it from the pod instead of the job.
> 
> Or are you changing your mind on what you're looking for?
> 
> If you just want to know if it succeeded or not, you can look at the job status conditions.

In fact, I plan to modify the klipper-helm image, which executes e.g. helm install xxx --wait, and the job becomes Succeeded only when the --wait execution completes.Based on this above, I would like to implement tracking of the job's status change by modifying the OnChange function to write the deployment completion status information into the HelmChart CR only when the job's status changes to Succeeded before writing the succeeded status information to the HelmChart CR.

> i don't know if everyone could get this work properly.
> 
> when the user interacts with something that will update the view, inside a $timeout, you may call the refresh function, that will re-evaluate queryselector. Now if you still need to work around with onBefore or onAfterchange, i don't think it's the best solutions for us.
> 
> A way to properly fix that, it's to rebind the clicks that happens on introjs, into the controller or service, then we'll make the changes that we need and after that we call ngIntroService.next().
> 
> if there's anyone in need i may provide that fix, just let me know.

## here's the logs

query i ran against my app to find these:

```sql
  SELECT
    msg_type_char(message_type, event_type) AS "T",
    log_timestamp,
    process_name,
    sender_process_name,
    process_id,
    thread_id,
    "category",
    subsystem,
    event_message
  FROM macos_system_logs
  WHERE log_timestamp > '2022-08-11'
    AND (
       process_id = 31265
    OR sender_process_name ~* 'processmonitor'
    OR process_name ~* 'processmonitor'
    OR event_message ~* 'processmonitor'
    )
  ORDER BY log_timestamp ASC
```

https://github.com/skiselkov/BetterPushb: The answer until there is a fix, is update settings in xplane 12 and then close the window without saving. This works but you have to do it each load of xplane 12. Or just leave settings at default.
Any updates on this? Having the same issue m1 Macbook Pro.  Mouse inputs for planning do not work, so pushback won't work.

> Any update on this? I cleared all preferences by deleting the preferences folder. Since then, BP does not work.
> Cannot rotate the aircraft with the mouse as above, and when hitting enter, the screen goes away, and there is no voice from the BP crew.

No, no news.  Only mouse rotation does not work for me, the rest works fine.  The plane is pushed out only in a straight line.
Just tried X-Plane 12 Beta 8 Compatibility Update again with the latest Zibo mod (4.3).  Now nothing shows up on the display when selecting the pre-plan pushback menu option.
Bitdefender has responded: "The files are clean and detection should be removed in the next couple of updates."
There is no BetterPushback initialization in this log. I can't really help you. My only suggestion is to try to update xplane or install more scenery.

Same issue here. It used to load without issue until I updated xplane to 11.53r1. Deleting the better pushback airport cache found here X-Plane 11\Output\caches\BetterPushbackAirports.cache fixed mine.
also I am running it on a windows system. with the latest update from Laminar.

Check "sim/graphics/view/field_of_view_horizontal_deg" & "sim/graphics/view/field_of_view_vertical_deg". BetterPushBack seems to want them to be zero since the vulcan version of x-plane 11 came out. You can either change in the x-plane 11 graphics settings or update with datareftool before opening betterpushback.

> Is there a chance to get a update ? Still waiting for an answer or an repair hint ....

If I understand it right, it might be a scenery problem. But in my case issue repeating in every airport...

Hi,
[Log.txt](https://github.com/skiselkov/BetterPushbackC/files/4455607/Log.txt)
 I'm having the same problem updated to V .48 for Vulkan 11.50 and every time I hit "Plan Pushback" the game crashes.

You don't need to change the UI scale. Keep it at 150%. 
Go into settings and check "Auto disconnect when done"
After that you can do everything using the drop down plugin menu. 
You don't need any buttons.
Even though it works this way, I hope that the Pushback Team updates to show the buttons in 150% UI Scale, or redo the drop down menu to make it more conducive to use without the buttons.

> I also had the audio initialization error after upgrading to the new version. I have figured out how to fix that. It seems a bit redundant, but I totally spaced on checking it and was getting irritated over the past week. After you update to the new version, MAKE SURE TO OPEN BP PREFERENCES FROM THE PLUGIN MENU AND RE-SELECT YOUR AUDIO DEVICES. I normally check that every time I change something that has audio, but this time I forgot, and yet it was the simplest answer to the problem I was having. Hope this helps everyone here.

Thanks, Yes I also managed to fix it by reselect audio.

In the [COMMANDS] section

## BP
BetterPushback/start = 0
BetterPushback/connect_first = 0
BetterPushback/disconnect = 0
BetterPushback/reconnect = 0

Now BP will work from Master to slave only

Hi!

I took a deeper look into this issue and tested the current provider behaviour.
It appears that the Terraform provider already supports SAML authentication requests signing.

There is no `signAuthnRequests` boolean in the SSO Settings API for SAML. 
Instead, signing is implicitly enabled when the following fields are configured:

- `certificate` + `private_key` or
- `certificate_path` + `private_key_path`

For example we can use this code in terraform:

```hcl
resource "grafana_sso_settings" "saml" {
  provider_name = "saml"

  saml_settings {
    enabled             = true
    certificate         = filebase64("${path.module}/sp.crt")
    private_key         = filebase64("${path.module}/sp.key")
    signature_algorithm = "rsa-sha256" # optional

    idp_metadata        = filebase64("${path.module}/idp-metadata.xml")
  }
}
```
or this one:

```hcl
resource "grafana_sso_settings" "saml" {
  provider_name = "saml"

  saml_settings {
    certificate_path    = "/certs/saml.crt"
    private_key_path    = "/certs/saml.key"
    signature_algorithm = "rsa-sha256" # optional

   idp_metadata        = filebase64("${path.module}/idp-metadata.xml")
  }
}
```


I’ve tested this against Grafana Cloud and verified it works as expected (SAML settings were updated successfully, and the configured signing fields are reflected in the UI).
Support for SAML (including these fields) was introduced in PR #1474.

Perhaps it would be helpful to explicitly mention in the documentation that configuring the certificate and private key enables SAML request signing.

As a summary:
- If a synthetics installation is setup via terraform everything will be working (checks will be running and sending data), but you won't be able to see it in the synthetics app, because the app is not 'initialized'
- The synthetics app initialization creates a synthetic-monitoring-datasource in Grafana. It also associates the metrics and logs datasource with the app.
- If the synthetic monitoring datasource can be created via terraform as well, it may be enough for the app to see itself as 'initialized' and display the data.  If that's true it may be enough to update the documentation for `grafana_synthetic_monitoring_installation`

Faced this when updated terraform code to delete few contactpoint/s which are being referred by existing notificationpolicy . 

commenting approach I am following for tacking this 

(Tl;dr : Run terraform twice . First tf run with extra var to remove reference of unused contactpoint from notificationpolicy , second tf run without extra var to destroy contactpoint)
Note : In our case , we don't have frequent deletions of contactpoints . So running tf apply twice for one off scenarios is fine.

PS : Tried running terraform by targetting only `grafana_notification_policy` resource , it still fails with `Error: [DELETE /v1/provisioning/contact-points/{UID}] DeleteContactpoints (status 409): {}` error.

_Excerpts_

- Added extra tf var 

```
# To deal with https://github.com/grafana/terraform-provider-grafana/issues/1806
# Removing existing contact points being refered by existing notification policy throws 409 error due to above issue of grafana terraform provider
# This variable is a one-time fix to resolve notification policy and contact point conflicts. Set to true to enable the fix when triggering deploy_alerts_* GH workflows by passing -var="onetimeFixforNotificationContactpointConflicts=true" under tf_arguments input
variable "onetimeFixforNotificationContactpointConflicts" {
  type        = bool
  description = "A one-time fix to resolve notification policy and contact point conflicts. Set to true to enable the fix & leave it afterwards as default is false."
  default     = false
}
```

- NotificationPolicy.tf

```
locals {
   has_slack_all_channel_id = var.slack_all_channel_id != "" ? true : false
}
resource "grafana_notification_policy" "default" {
  provider = grafana.alert
  # disable_provenance = true
  disable_provenance = local.disable_provenance
  contact_point      = "grafana-default-email"
  group_by           = ["..."]
------
------
  dynamic "policy" {
    for_each = local.has_slack_all_channel_id ? [1] : []
    content {
      contact_point = grafana_contact_point.slack[0].name 
      group_by      = ["..."]

      matcher {
        label = "slack"
        match = "="
        value = "true"
      }

      continue = true
      }
  }

  group_wait      = "30s"
  group_interval  = "5m"
  repeat_interval = "4d3h"
```

- ContactPoints.tf

```
# Contact Point : Slack - All Alerts Channel
resource "grafana_contact_point" "slack" {
  count = var.onetimeFixforNotificationContactpointConflicts ? 1 : (local.has_slack_all_channel_id ? 1 : 0)
  name     = "Grafana-Slack-Alerts"
  provider = grafana.alert
  # If we want to disable provenance(allowing modifications from UI)
  # disable_provenance = true
  disable_provenance = local.disable_provenance

  slack {
    token     = var.slack_token
    recipient = var.onetimeFixforNotificationContactpointConflicts ? "somedummychannelid" : var.slack_all_channel_id
---------
----------
  }
}
```

- TfVars

dev.tfvar
```
slack_all_channel_id    = ""
```

prod.tfvar
```

```

This is intended behaviour, per the Terraform Provider, which will always destroy resources before updating, regardless of the inferred dependency graph. _However_, this can be solved by adding the following block to your contact point resource: ``` resource "grafana_contact_point" "contact_points" { .... lifecycle { create_before_destroy = true } .... } ``` This works because it forces dependant resources to be updated, **before** attempting to destroy the contact point. This means that the notification policy will be modified first, then the contact point will be destroyed after (See attached screenshot). **Gotcha:** In order for this to work, you'll need to succesfully run an apply _**before**_ you add the lifecycle argument. This is because you get a chicken and egg scenario, where the lifecycle argument is never added, because it tries to delete the contact point first still. To get round this, we manually jumped on our grafana-alerting instance, and used the grafana-api to delete the current notification policy. Before immediately running an apply. Source: See the explanation from @jbardin [in this issue in the Terraform Provider](https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform/issues/25010#issuecomment-651143212)

https://github.com/mjhydri/BeatNet/issue:
I'm sort of there inside docker at least, using my own repo that just removes the version numbers on librosa and numba...

Here is the current docker:

```docker
FROM ubuntu:22.04
# avoid stuck build due to user prompt
ARG DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

RUN apt-get update -y
RUN apt-get install libasound-dev libportaudio2 libportaudiocpp0 portaudio19-dev libsndfile1 -y
RUN apt-get install -y wget 
RUN apt-get install software-properties-common git -y
RUN add-apt-repository ppa:deadsnakes/ppa -y
RUN apt update
RUN apt-get install -y python3.8 python3.8-dev python3.8-venv build-essential 
RUN apt-get clean && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

RUN bash -c 'wget --no-check-certificate -O - https://apt.llvm.org/llvm-snapshot.gpg.key' | apt-key add -
RUN add-apt-repository 'deb http://apt.llvm.org/jammy/ llvm-toolchain-jammy main'
RUN apt-get install llvm-11* -y

RUN python3.8 -m venv /venv
ENV PATH=/venv/bin:$PATH

RUN pip3 install --no-cache-dir soundfile
RUN pip3 install --no-cache-dir numpy
RUN pip3 install --no-cache-dir numba
RUN pip3 install --no-cache-dir Pyaudio
RUN pip3 install --no-cache-dir cython
RUN pip3 install --no-cache-dir git+https://github.com/aphillipo/BeatNet

CMD ["python"]
```

To run it add the above to a directory in a file called `Dockerfile` and then run the following:

```bash
docker build -t beatnet-dockerised .
docker run --rm --device /dev/null:/dev/snd -it beatnet-dockerised
```

This will drop you into a python shell that might be able to run the offline models - the streaming ones seem to have an ASLA crash that I don't understand.

Anyway this is at least a start, it would be good for the author to consider checking if the packages he depends on specific versions for have been fixed because they cause incompatibility on MacOS and make things very hard to build. I'll leave mounting a volume for music to read from as an exercise for the reader...

@blacklistisnotracist - A few months ago, an NCSC customer contacted me to ask if we would consider making a small but significant change to some of the wording we use on the NCSC website. When she asked the question, I immediately smacked myself in the head for not thinking of it a long time ago. And I was really glad to say: yes, we will make this change straight away, and I'm sorry you had to come and ask us to do it.

It's fairly common to say whitelisting and blacklisting to describe desirable and undesirable things in cyber security. For instance, when talking about which applications you will allow or deny on your corporate network; or deciding which bad passwords you want your users not to be able to use.

However, there's an issue with the terminology. It only makes sense if you equate white with 'good, permitted, safe' and black with 'bad, dangerous, forbidden'. There are some obvious problems with this. So in the name of helping to stamp out racism in cyber security, we will avoid this casually pejorative wording on our website in the future. No, it's not the biggest issue in the world - but to borrow a slogan from elsewhere: every little helps.

You may not see why this matters. If you're not adversely affected by racial stereotyping yourself, then please count yourself lucky. For some of your colleagues (and potential future colleagues), this really is a change worth making. From now on, the NCSC will use 'allow list' and 'deny list' in place of 'whitelist' and 'blacklist' on our website. Which, in fact, is clearer and less ambiguous. So as well as being more inclusive of all, this is a net benefit to our web content. We are editing our guidance across the website to update the terms, but if you do spot any in the meantime then please do contact us.

I hope that if you're seeking to make this, or similar changes in your own organisation, this blog post helps you to convince people around you that it's worth doing. And finally, a word from the NCSC's Technical Director Ian Levy (supported by the full NCSC Management Board): "If you’re thinking about getting in touch saying this is political correctness gone mad, don’t bother."



Emma W
Head of Advice and Guidance, NCSC
https://www.ncsc.gov.uk/blog-post/terminology-its-not-black-and-white

Hi @marufmax. I solved it. There is a description:

1) You should have API_KEY, but not any API_KEY. It has to be API_KEY with proper scope - scope to places google api.

To get it you should go to page

> http://g.co/dev/maps-no-account
 
Select one of the options:

- maps
- routes
- places

In our case places, but you can select more if you want to have more universal API_KEY.

Next, connect it with your account. But look at pricing.

> https://developers.google.com/places/web-service/usage-and-billing

Before changes prices were the following:

Standard Usage Limits for Places API
--
Standard Plan quota is calculated as the sum of client-side and server-side requests.
+ Starts at 1,000 free requests per 24 hour period.
+ Get 150,000 free requests per day if you enable billing to verify your identity.
+ Rate limit is 100 requests per second. It is calculated as the sum of client-side and server-side requests for all applications using the credentials of the same 
+ Standard Plan project. Additional rate limit applied per user session, regardless of how many users share the same project.

Now pricing is changed. The most interesting part here:

![screenshot from 2019-01-24 21-44-41](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/16663028/51707352-4da24300-2021-11e9-91ee-bbd165d3ced0.png)

I worried about the article

> https://www.reddit.com/r/webdev/comments/8vrkm6/google_places_api_new_pricing_from_0_to_2180_per/

It means that when you start using this package and update an address on any key pressed you should consider about 5 - 20 requests for one usage of this component by the user. In worst case 1000 usages mean

20 * 32 USD.

Finally, I resigned from this component and stop using it after changes in pricing, but what you will do it is your decision. In my case, I come back to geocoding api that is cheapest and works like a charm. 

> https://developers.google.com/maps/documentation/geocoding/intro

# Question 

Can be 134 line of 

> https://github.com/olefirenko/vue-google-autocomplete/blob/master/src/VueGoogleAutocomplete.vue

changed to use Geocoding?

I anyway will no use this component and tyme my own, but for further users, pricing issue can make a big difference.

Ran into this issue as well, albeit by accidentally renaming a tenant to another existing tenant's name with tenants:update.  Your method of then fixing the other tenant worked, thanks.

Once the tasks accepts names instead of (or in addition to) generated IDs, the `tenants:add` task could be extended to also allow editing existing tenants (e.g. change the secret). This is currently not possible at all.

I'd replace the `tenants:add[name,secrets]` task with a single independent `tenants:set[name,secrets]` task that creates or updates a given tenant. This greatly simplifies scripting and automated deployments.

**Edit:**
If the ID is important (e.g. to be able to change the tenants name later without loosing any state) then `tenants:set` should take three arguments: `tenants:set[id,name,secrets]` and the `id` should be user-defined, not auto-generated, for new tenants.

The issue is that the getMeetingInfo which makes use of the redis database for its response, is being performed before the poller finished its update. And getRecordings which is based on the getRecordings response received from the actual BBB servers may be responding faster than the poller.

While this would work normally fine with small deployments, it won't scale. 

Bottom line the getRecordings response should be based on the info contained in the local redis database as well.

The poller(s) should be the only one(s) sending requests to the BBB servers for fetching data.
